prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis increasing in urban sleep clinics

vulnerablePopulations: patients without access to PSG/MSLT testing
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical EDS with cataplexy and confirmation by PSG + MSLT where available; diagnosis delays are common

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to limited sleep lab access and medication costs

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Sleep labs concentrated in urban tertiary centers; many cases remain undiagnosed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Scheduled naps, sleep hygiene, stimulants/wake-promoting medications where available; manage comorbid sleep disorders
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited PSG/MSLT availability and high out-of-pocket costs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing sleep medicine services in major cities
